Study Guide
  • Civil Rights Legal Victories and Direct Action
  • Reconstruction and the Rise of Jim Crow

Civil Rights Legal Victories and Direct Action

The Civil Rights Movement employed both legal strategies and nonviolent direct action to challenge segregation. The NAACP's legal efforts culminated in Brown v. Board of Education (1954), which overturned the doctrine of separate but equal, while grassroots movements like the Montgomery Bus Boycott demonstrated the power of organized community resistance.

  • Montgomery Bus Boycott A 381-day protest (1955–1956) sparked by Rosa Parks's arrest and organized by the Montgomery Improvement Association under Martin Luther King Jr.'s leadership, demonstrating the effectiveness of nonviolent direct action in challenging segregation.

What Supreme Court decision overturned the 'separate but equal' doctrine in 1954?

How long did the Montgomery Bus Boycott last, and who led the Montgomery Improvement Association?

Sources

  • Darling — The Quest for Equality

Reconstruction and the Rise of Jim Crow

After the Civil War, formerly enslaved people sought freedom through political participation, land ownership, and education. However, the sharecropping system replaced slavery with a new form of economic bondage, trapping Black farmers in cycles of debt and limiting their ability to achieve true independence.

  • Forty Acres and a Mule A promise of land redistribution to formerly enslaved people that was largely abandoned, leaving most freedpeople without economic resources or property ownership.
  • Jim Crow Laws Laws that enforced racial segregation in public life—schools, transportation, restaurants, restrooms—throughout the South from the 1870s through the 1960s, creating a system of legal discrimination.
Booker T. Washington
Founded the Tuskegee Institute in 1881, emphasizing vocational training and economic self-sufficiency over immediate political equality as the path forward for Black Americans.
W.E.B. Du Bois
Co-founded the NAACP in 1909 and argued for full and immediate civil and political rights of Black Americans, directly challenging Washington's accommodationist approach.

What did the Reconstruction Acts of 1867 require Southern states to do before readmission to the Union?

What constitutional doctrine did Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) establish regarding racial segregation?

Select A

Sources

  • Darling — The Quest for Equality

You're done!
